claim_text: The Rotterdam criteria should be used for diagnosing PCOS.
evidence_text: We suggest using the Rotterdam criteria for diagnosing PCOS (presence of two of the following criteria: androgen excess, ovulatory dysfunction, or polycystic ovaries).

claim_text: Hormonal contraceptives are the first-line management for menstrual abnormalities in PCOS.
evidence_text: Hormonal contraceptives are the first-line management for menstrual abnormalities and hirsutism/acne in PCOS.

claim_text: Clomiphene is the first-line therapy for infertility in PCOS.
evidence_text: Clomiphene is currently the first-line therapy for infertility.

claim_text: Metformin is beneficial for metabolic/glycemic abnormalities in PCOS.
evidence_text: Metformin is beneficial for metabolic/glycemic abnormalities and for improving menstrual irregularities.

claim_text: The Endocrine Society announced that PCOS would henceforth be known as PMOS.
evidence_text: The Endocrine Society, a global network of people dealing with hormones, announced that PCOS (Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome) would henceforth be known as PMOS (Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome).

claim_text: The term PCOS was deemed 'inaccurate' as it relied only on the presence of ovarian cysts.
evidence_text: The authors of the paper Helena J. Teede et al argued that the term PCOS was ‘inaccurate’ as it relied only on the presence of ovarian cysts.

claim_text: PCOS affects an estimated 170 million women during their reproductive years.
evidence_text: The significance lies not only in the fact that the definition has broadened the panel of factors that will facilitate better diagnosis, but also that PCOS affects a staggering number of women — it is estimated that the syndrome affects 170 million women during their reproductive years alone.

claim_text: The PCOS Awareness Association raises global awareness of PCOS.
evidence_text: Our mission at the PCOS Awareness Association is to raise global awareness of Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS).

claim_text: The organization provides educational services to those affected by PCOS.
evidence_text: We strive to break down barriers and offer educational services with the help of our dedicated volunteers.

claim_text: PCOS is being renamed to PMOS.
evidence_text: A disorder that affects 170 million women worldwide and is the leading cause of infertility, known as polycystic ovary syndrome, or PCOS, is being renamed to improve diagnosis and care.

claim_text: PCOS has been renamed to PMOS to better reflect its complex nature.
evidence_text: the condition, previously known as Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), will now be called Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS

claim_text: The new name reflects the condition’s broader impact across multiple systems.
evidence_text: Experts say the new name reflects the condition’s broader impact across the endocrine, metabolic and reproductive systems

claim_text: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) affects an estimated 10–13% of reproductive-aged women.
evidence_text: PCOS affects an estimated 10–13% of reproductive-aged women.

claim_text: Up to 70% of women with PCOS worldwide do not know they have this condition.

claim_text: PCOS is the most common cause of anovulation among women globally.
evidence_text: PCOS is the most common cause of anovulation among women globally and a leading cause of infertility.

claim_text: PCOS is a chronic metabolic condition that persists beyond the reproductive years.

evidence_text: The Endocrine Society...has decided to rename Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) as Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS).

claim_text: The National Health Policy, 2017 aims for universal access to good quality health care services.
evidence_text: The aim of NHM is to ensure progress towards the goals and targets set out in the National Health Policy, 2017 for ensuring 'universal access to equitable, affordable and quality health care services'.

claim_text: The National Health Policy, 2017 promotes health and well-being for all ages.
evidence_text: The National Health Policy, 2017 envisages- 'the attainment of the highest possible level of health and wellbeing for all at all ages'.

claim_text: The National Health Policy, 2017 aligns with Sustainable Development Goal 3.
evidence_text: This would also enable the country to advance towards meeting Sustainable Development Goal3, i.e. 'Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages'.

claim_text: The National Institute for Health Research (NIHR) was established on 27 June 1984.
evidence_text: The National Institute for Health Research (NIHR), Jodhpur, was established on 27 June 1984 as the Desert Medicine Research Centre (DMRC).

claim_text: NIHR was renamed on 7 December 2019.
evidence_text: It was later renamed the National Institute for Implementation Research on Non-Communicable Diseases (NIIRNCD) on 7 December 2019.

claim_text: NIHR was redesignated on 29 April 2026.
evidence_text: subsequently redesignated as NIHR on 29 April 2026.

claim_text: NIHR focuses on non-communicable disease (NCD) risk factors.
evidence_text: NIHR focuses on generating high-impact research across priority domains, strengthening capacity through specialized training programmes, and developing effective behaviour change communication strategies to address non-communicable disease (NCD) risk factors.

claim_text: The trial found no difference in meaningful outcomes between two fluid treatment options for pediatric sepsis.
evidence_text: A large clinical trial supported by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) compared two commonly used treatments for pediatric patients treated for septic shock and found no difference in meaningful outcomes.

claim_text: Donald Trump postponed a planned military attack on Iran after it sent a peace proposal to Washington.
evidence_text: Donald Trump said he postponed a planned military attack on Iran set for Tuesday after it sent a peace proposal to Washington.
